title: baccharoides calvoana subsp. meridionalis
intro: Baccharoides calvoana subsp. meridionalis, also known as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, is a plant native to the Philippines. It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. It is commonly prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk medicine for its antioxidant and antiseptic properties.
